reviews.review_text: This software is free. Buying it on here is supporting the developers and giving yourself an hour counter. It's impossible for me to not recommend this.
Support art by real artists, for real artists, universally and forever.
You can always download this app for free since it's open-source, but I decided to support the developers because they created such a great art program. Highly recommend giving it a try <3

I've been using Krita for about seven years now and it's my favourite digital art tool, I got it on Steam recently as a kind of formality, realistically speaking I probably spent over a thousand hours using Krita over those years
Amazing drawing app for a very cheap price. The platform is similar to some high-end classed drawing apps, except it is actually affordable and doesn't lack in features.
Speenchless, it was the best program, I've ever found for editing, drawing, and other things, it was so much fun!

its just plane good, used it instead of photoshop for my diploma and now its my primary art program

edit1: before this i used clip studio paint

Krita - Is the tool that brings me joy, inspires me, I guess.
This tool giving me some unusual association, It brings me on the past which I like so much and wanna feel those emotions again and again..
Great drawing program with neat letterboxing features for comics, manga and comic style graphics. Generally very smart layering and correction options and good use of hotkeys by default.

The newest version improved a lot of things that were behind other art softeare.
Now, the Popup Palette could have the Color Sample on it since it's a very nescesary tool while drawing.
its pretty simple, but id like it more if certain canvases saved the workspaces used on them so i wouldn't have to change my workspace for animation/drawing just because i went to a diff canvas

Ngl, best art program I've ever used. The UI feels clean, everything is organized, AND you get to animate too?
10/10 in my book. That's not even including the brushes, tools, etc, it has alot of great options, and you can download custom brushes! (I've never downloaded custom brushes, but ik alot of people like to)

If you're new, the program can be VERY overwhelming, especially with all the buttons that it presents you with, but trust me, once you get over that learning curve, it's smooth sailing from there.

Definitely recommend this art program.

Over-all summary; Overwhelming as a noobie, but once you get used to it,  it's an AMAZING art program

It's an opensource and FREE software, which I've actually grown to love in comparison to other softwares. I decided to support it simply because its a really nice and accessible program for any aspiring artist.
Best art software I've ever used (so far/as of yet), so yes you can assume I definitely recommend. It isn't free here but if you draw and you use Krita then you can support the developers.
Good. Even if it is free, I still made the decision to buy it since it keeps track of the time spent here. Also a way to support the devs.

Deeply versatile graphics application that can handle digital painting, vector, animation, and comics. Subscription-free, freely available if you don't want to buy it on Steam. Multi-platform means it is basically the same app across whatever devices you have.

10/10
paid $10 for a software I already had to support the krita team, and just to track my hours on steam.
works wonderfully, I highly suggest getting if you're a beginner or even advanced artist and don't have it.
good app i really liked using it off the krita website and wanted to contribute to it so when i saw it on steam for 8 pound GBP i thought it was a steal so i bought it and im loving it so far

I decided to buy it on Steam after looking into 5.3 features lol. Great update.
The transition wasn't silky smooth though, had to import my settings and resources manually from a flatpak installation.

90% of the time I'm doomscrolling on my phone

what a wonderful program!! versatile, tons of tools, very customizable, and animation!! it's definitely intimidating to look at for new users, but its held up beautifully for me!! its free, but I think it's well worth it to buy it :]
I absolutely enjoy using Krita, have been using since 2020 for freeeee! I purposefully bought it just to support the community of krita! all the best.

Been using for 5 years already and finally support Krita team with my money tysm for be the one in my heart of the software i use for creating my art for applying for university and be the one of my best friends of a man in the time i feel low and burnout from classes and everything. I have this software to let me create and spread my gayass old men yaoi arts to the world for everyone who needs. thank you so much for being in my every happy moment and successful moment
Kirta, very cool and good program, but to the uttermost is hard.

got krita from the official website before switching to steam after a year or 2 to support the devs! absolutely worth it, just proves that you dont need some "fancy" program to draw on.
if you plan on drawing digital, definitely try krita out
Giga chads use open source software, losers use adobe suite.

Krita offers diverse and efficient tools for literally everyone. Some tools, like the bucket fill, are kinda strange to use, in my opinion, but other than that, I found out that there are tools for comic book artists.

Some stock brushes can be a little hard to work with, but we are blessed that this is open-source, which means we can alter and add our own brushes to the software, as well as have the freedom to do anything to it. Krita can look hard, but a couple of tutorial videos actually help you get the idea of how the fundamentals work.

I definitely recommend buying this on sale, since it has Steam's cloud save and consistent updates and fixes.

I have been using Krita for a very long time now, at the very least a couple of years. I finally bit the bullet and bought the Steam version for the automatic updates. What a fantastic software, genuinely. I adore the brushes, how it feels to draw, I even appreciate the animation dockers. Krita can be a little overwhelming to understand when you are a beginner, but thankfully everything is customizable and upon hover each tool will tell you what it is. It's very quick to get the hang of and learn. I would highly highly recommend Krita. Don't feel pressured to buy the steam version, I must have atleast 1000 hours in the free version that you can download on the web. The steam version is just here for the auto updates and if you want to support Krita. Either way, if you are looking for a drawing software, Krita is a fantastic option :D
phenomenal art program and as an added bonus the devs don't even gorge themselves on your data like vampires leeching off your soul :)
its awesome app for geeks and comic book artists like me lol
goated software. yes its free online but support them for their work if you can!

decidi comprar a versão da steam mesmo não usando a versão grátis, tanto porque queria ver as minhas horas de desenho quanto mudei do Windows pro Linux e Clip Studio Paint não oferece suporte (usava o CSP desde 2020/2021).
por mais que usei por "pouco" tempo (também pq pra desenhar 26 horas não é absolutamente nada KKKK), de início achei esquisito por costume em outras plataformas, mas posso dizer que gastei meu dinheiro em um aplicativo ótimo de desenho! os pincéis já default são muito bons, e a capacidade de customizar vários aspectos de como você usa o aplicativo (incluindo o uso de plugins) genuinamente ajuda bastante a criar algo específico para você. 
não tenho um laptop bom no presente momento, tanto que até o CSP travava em alguns pontos de usagem (usei para converter meus arquivos de desenhos antigos para o .kra), mas o krita me parece leve o suficiente que não tenho muitos problemas em usar ele, tirando alguns pontos mais atribuídos ao driver da mesa digitalizadora que estou usando no presente momento (OpenTabletDriver).
de qualquer maneira, definitivamente deveria ter mudado pro krita faz anos, um aplicativo de desenho muito poderoso que ganha em alguns aspectos (na minha opinião) em relação ao CSP. recomendaria mais comprar a versão da steam se querer contar as horas de uso, ou para ajudar os desenvolvedores!
Powerful, Opensource, Awesome. Would recommend getting for free on the site, if you wanna support the devs, grab it here on steam.

As a hobbyist - it was fantastic to have free, powerful software to sink my teeth into. I've been able to progress my skills faster than expected, and love to see the direction the krita is team is taking the software. More importantly, I love to see the direction our species is taking open source tools (blender, gimp, etc).
And giving them a little $ to have updates handled by steam seems like a wonderful arrangement. Good stuff!

I love Krita so much, Im surprised it's free but these developers deserve my money so might aswell buy it on here. 
The new steam cloud compatibility is also really cool and very handy.
It can often stutter when using larger brushes but that's the issue with most art software so I can't really hold it against Krita specifically. (Or maybe my hardware just isn't good enough, which is pretty likely)
ilk kez dijital çizime başlamak için hem çok kolay bir kullanımı var hem de çok fazla özelliğe sahip

Works well as a photoshop replacement specifically for digital art instead of photo editing. Has a fair bit of customization you can do to get the workspaces to look how you like.

Has good 3rd party digital brush pack support and good support for 3rd party python scripts to extend what is possible in the program, in case you need it. Filters work fine and G'MIC-QT filters work well as well when the normal filters don't work as well for what you need. Would not recommend passing off the work of the G'MIC-QT filters as something you did, but they're still useful. Works well enough with touch screens and graphics tablets.

Default brush packs are basic but get the job done. Would recommend going into "manage resource libraries" under settings and enabling krita 3 default resource bundle for more base brushes if you don't want to use 3rd party brushes/brush packs.

All in all, it's a pretty good program
Hella great for drawing, it's the best art software out there. 10 bucks is a win or on sale! ++10/10
altho i love this app and i even bought 10 buck version instead using the free one, the audio import still aint workin for me please for the love of god help me
never used a digital art program before. This was pretty easy to learn and get the hang of! As a beginner I appreciate that
Impressive digital painting software that is proffessional grade and free 10/10

It's ok.
GIMP still has a better selection tool but I do like the brushes more in Krita.
Wanted to draw furries, got Krita, drew furries, now I own furries drawings, thanks Krita!
Very solid app for drawing, i've easily been able to use this without any issues although i am still a beginner. the UI is simple and not clustered everywhere, And i have a feel for Renby....
My daughter has loved been able to let her creativity flow with this application. happy to support the developers!

Too complicated of a tool. Needless prebuilt features, no legible optimization, and the documentation is so large that it is like reading through a phone book for a single thing that should have been easy. I could never even figure out to effectively alter brushes, and even worse, the palette board wasn't even working, it was automatically disabled??? I had to manually set this up. Completely inferior tool compared to aseprite. Aseprite is easy to understand and super user friendly. This program has zero work flow and I just can't use a cluttered mess. I cannot recommend and honestly I am really sorry but I'd give this a 1/10.

AWESOME SOFTWARE, PLEASE CHANGE THE FONT SELECTION IT'S TERRIBLE

it just scrolls past what I'm trying to look at and then suddenly teleports back to the top 😭

The best drawing software (it is on their website), I am willing to pay.
Thanks your work on Krita!

I've been using Krita since 2014, and I have to say it's the best painting software I've ever encountered! The blending options are SO good, and its features are just easy and intuitive.
I've animated in it, made timelapses and multiple illustrations.

While it's free to download on Krita's web page (Yes, you can try it without spending a single coin, for as long as you like!) I'm happy to financially support the incredible work put into making this amazing software - and enjoy the auto-updates via Steam.
Very good drawing software, you can get it free online as it is open source but I like supporting the devs and that it shows my hours on Steam so I got it here too
Лучшее приложение для редактирования изображений. Долго пользовался бесплатной версией. Платная была куплена только для того чтобы копить часы в ней :D

Does not support graphic tablets i guess, the in app tablet settings dont work, every window in app (and dialogue box) pops up on the 2nd monitor not on the tablet. I dont have the patience to troubleshoot this junk.

Light-weight, open source Photoshop. 
It's free but I don't mind the $10 donation for the tracker since that's something I've always struggled to do with the roughest of estimates.

I very much enjoy Krita.... Before even buying it outright on Steam, I think I've for sure spent many many hundreds of hours on this program since I began drawing (that would have been roughly 2 1/2 years ago). It is available for free and has almost everything you could possibly want with the only downside being a little bit of a difficulty to learn without prior knowledge to other similar programs such as Photoshop.

Offers a wide range of possibilities for artistic expression. 
Strongly recommended!
Free normally, but pitching in a few dollars makes me feel better about how long I've used this for free.

I've been drawing digitally for 5 years, and I've been using Krita since the very start.


What I like about this app is that I can customize everything to my preference and get all the brushes I need & want, and it costs absolutely NOTHING.

Krita is completely free on their website, and the reason I bought it today was mainly so that I could count every single hour I spend everyday drawing. I've probably spent over 2,000 hours on this app and I intend on having that count go higher, this time actually showing my progress.

My friends can finally see how much I no-life this app when I'm not playing games.

Another plus is that I'm pretty sure the Krita on Steam auto-updates? Which is pretty cool if there are any bug-fixes, cause I've been using an old Krita client for the past 2 years.

I also bought it to give support to an app I use all the time. I keep on discovering new features, and the Krita community can make some really good brushes for me to use. I've managed to make over 200$ from a free app, so giving 5$ for an hour counter isn't a problem at all.


Krita has been the #1 app I've recommended to new artists because it's so easy to use.
At first, they said it was overwhelming with how much things are on the screen, but then I spent 5 minutes showing them how to customize their UI to their needs, where to get new brushes, and "lifehacks" that they explained they wanted to do but couldn't or didn't know how in other apps. Literally only 5 minutes is all it takes for me to convince them to use Krita, and they've been using it ever since.

Again, buying Krita on Steam is 100% optional. I'm only doing so to leave my thoughts on the app cause I don't really review stuff, nor do I know where to leave reviews for things outside of steam.
worth 6 dollar spent (if yall even pirating ts cuz of 6 dollars yall broke asf)

I liked it :D
Lightweight, no ads, no popups, just one time purchase open source app

Would recommend this to especially hobby level artists like myself. 

Go use Clip Studio Paint or Photoshop if you have the specific need or are working in a pipeline. if you just draw/paint, I recommend giving Krita a fair shot (download for free on their website) and see if you vibe with it.

💔Good app for drawing💔
💔The only big downside is that you can't take a screenshot of your drawing to upload to steam💔
Good app, must support devs nothing else
ive been using it for the past 3 hours its such a good upgrade from paint.net

fire software, i got it after getting a new pen and it's been great. it has its flaws and bugs occasionally, but they rarely detract from the utility of the software. 9/10 would recommend, not even mad i missed the sale when buying it
fastest and better then photoshop !  worth it!

I'm a game dev and I've been using this for years, finally bought it on steam to support the devs. This is fantastic for literally anything. Image editing, digital art, animation, texturing for games. Better than photoshop, and infinitely cheaper

After making digital art for 10 years, and using many programs, this one I recommend the most. After using the free version for ages I decided to pay for it because I do want to give them my money for such a high quality program. Even if you're a beginner, I recommend learning to use it as it is worth in the long run, 10/10.

Really solid drawing app. Some features don't feel as complete, like animation and working with SVGs are a bit finicky and not as optimized, but it has a large enough community where you can ask something online and you'll probably get a good answer in a reasonable amount of time. 

I'm a proud Krita user and I love teaching people how to use it.

So so happy I found this app when I did. Sent photoshop to hell and never looked back, this is perfect for illustration! Was happy to pay for the paid version here on steam to support the devs and to keep digital illustration affordable. 10/10.
